notes: Gravity for Ember & Ash. The phone rings; the foreman wants a decision; the AC dies for the afternoon; somebody on the crew is talking to somebody they shouldn't be. The endgame cards bring the older man in the charcoal suit. Specificity, not animism — the office is hot because it is August and Jimmy installed the AC; the silt rises because Antonio has been letting the phone ring for fourteen days.

deck.title: the phone buzzes again
deck.flavor: Q. PAUL on the screen. twenty-seven seconds to voicemail. you do not look at the screen. you know what it says.

deck.title: the AC unit complains
deck.flavor: the window unit jimmy installed kicks down to half. melting plastic. the office gets a degree warmer in the time it takes to notice.

deck.title: the foreman wants a decision
deck.flavor: he is on the stair with a clipboard. the beam can go up tonight. it will not be straight. you can have it tonight or you can have it straight. you cannot have both.

deck.title: the crew radio changes
deck.flavor: spanish to salsa. somebody on the crew turned it up. somebody else turned it down. it stays at the level the crew agreed on.

deck.title: lila emails three lines
deck.flavor: subject: re: ember. three lines in the body. somebody is courting her with a better offer.

deck.title: jimmy's truck pulls up
deck.flavor: the f-150. the radio on. he gets out. he is on the back stair in two minutes.

deck.title: the hammer finds its rhythm
deck.flavor: the slow bored hammer downstairs finds the rhythm hammers find when the hand that holds them has decided to be there for the rest of the afternoon.

deck.title: the carpet path
deck.flavor: you walk the office. eight strides corner to corner. you have walked this route, by your count, somewhere north of six thousand times.

deck.title: the brick with a date
deck.flavor: you pick up a brick from the salvage pile. 1947 stamped into it. you put it in your coat pocket. you have been putting things in your coat pocket for nine months.

deck.title: the gas line problem returns
deck.flavor: wrong gauge. you can retrofit the equipment, expensive. you can retrofit the line, also expensive plus a permit you do not have. the third option is not, today, on the table.

deck.title: your thumb above the phone
deck.flavor: you find your father's number. you hold your thumb above it. eight seconds. nine. ten. eleven. you set the phone back down.

deck.title: the sidewalk in august
deck.flavor: you step out front. the heat comes up through the soles of your shoes. the marigny smells the way the marigny smells in august.

deck.title: the corner across the street
deck.flavor: an older man in a charcoal suit. lawyer-shoes. lapels smoothed. he is across the street. he is smoking. he is watching the office stair.

deck.title: the sedan is back
deck.flavor: a dark sedan. parked in the shadow of the dumpster, beyond the reach of any camera you have. the engine off. the windows up.

deck.title: the day holds
deck.flavor: the cypress beam has not come down. the gas line is the gas line. the phone is the phone. you are still in the office. that is the win today.
